_extraction_notes.school_list_gap: The CSPD 2023 Annual Report does NOT contain a complete enumerated list of all 80 schools. School names were extracted only from incidental references throughout the document (VET awards, HSC results, teacher recruitment stories) plus capital grants listed in the CSNSW report. The school-names.json file contains 18 confirmed school names — a partial list only. A full school directory is not present in either PDF and must be sourced from ACARA or the CSPD website.

strategic_priorities.detail: Derived from 2023 NAPLAN data — emphasis on shifting students from 'Strong' to 'Exceeding' proficiency, particularly in Numeracy
Identified as a system priority following NAPLAN analysis
Identified as a system priority following NAPLAN analysis

strategic_priorities.priority: Catholic formation — Life in Abundance framework
strategic_priorities.detail: Formation Foundation Document launched in 2023 titled 'Life in Abundance: Human Flourishing in the Way of Jesus', developed by collaboration led by Prof. Br David Hall. Supported by additional release time for Religious Education Coordinators, Faith in Action Team Coordinators for every school community, and piloting of Directors of Catholic Identity and Mission in 13 school communities.

Wellbeing Directorate in second full year (2023): 140+ specialist teachers, social workers and psychologists. Staff Wellbeing Policy and Framework launched May 2023. Student Wellbeing Framework released July 2023 — five themes: Leadership, Inclusion, Partnerships with Parish and Family, Support, Student Voice.

CSPD Infrastructure 10-Year Capital Framework developed 2023. Focus areas: equity (access to excellent facilities), growth (rising enrolment), Universal Preschool preparation, support services. Business cases and master plans for 28 schools commenced late 2023.

Board letter states: 'We move into 2024 with an even greater sense of common direction and purpose that will be crystallised in the development of a new organisation-wide strategy.' No further detail available in the 2023 report.

strategic_priorities.priority: Early Careers Engagement — Teacher Scholarship Program
strategic_priorities.detail: 2024 CSPD Teacher Scholarship Program announced late 2023 targeting current Year 12 students pursuing teaching degrees. 102 inaugural recipients. $5,000/year tuition support plus laptop. Part of a broader Early Careers Engagement strategy.

CSPD became incorporated company limited by guarantee from 1 January 2023. New name (from CEDP), new Board of Directors replacing Diocesan Schools Council, new Executive Director Jack de Groot. Three Board committees: Audit and Risk; Mission, Learning and People; Infrastructure and Property.
Innovate RAP in development. Cultural and Ceremonial Leave Policy introduced for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander employees. Two dedicated RAP roles established in People & Culture Directorate.

cspd_report.uplift_details: No reference to an 'Uplift Framework' by that name found in the CSPD 2023 Annual Report. School improvement mechanisms described include: annual school improvement plans tied to the Formation Foundation Document (p.32); NAPLAN proficiency data driving teaching methodology priorities (p.22); Tell Them From Me (TTFM) surveys as primary customer experience and school improvement indicator — sixth consecutive year in 2023 (p.37); Employee Engagement Survey (p.38). The Wellbeing Directorate (p.33-35) and the 10-Year Capital Framework (p.36) are the main system-level uplift mechanisms described. A discrete 'Uplift Framework' may be a 2024+ initiative not yet reported in this document.

new_schools.detail: St John Paul II College, Schofields listed in CSNSW 2024 CBGA capital grants (Parramatta diocese) — $16.5m total project, $11.6m grant. Schofields is a high-growth area in north-west Sydney, suggesting new or significantly expanded school.

CSPD Infrastructure 10-Year Capital Framework references 'Growth: Accommodating rising enrolment and catering to future needs' and 'Universal Preschool: Preparing for the implementation' as key investment areas. Business cases for 28 schools commenced late 2023. No specific new school openings or closures named in the 2023 annual report.

challenges.detail: The 2023 NAPLAN dataset shed light on the imperative for CSPD to increase the transition of students from 'Strong' to 'Exceeding', particularly in the area of Numeracy.

challenges.challenge: Major organisational transformation in a single year
challenges.detail: 2023 was 'a year of significant change for the organisation, with a new governance model and Board, a new name and branding, and a new Executive Director.' Organisation-wide strategy not yet developed — to be crystallised in 2024.
Employee engagement score stable at 69%. Areas of focus: increasing presence and visibility of System Leaders, enhancing communication, work-life balance, wellbeing for staff.

challenges.challenge: Gender equity gap in senior leadership
challenges.detail: Room for improvement in the upper quartile of the workforce (primarily leadership roles). Key management personnel: 40% female, 60% male. Appointments into management roles trending positive (70% women in reporting period).
New positive duty to eliminate sexual harassment in 2023. System Leaders required to complete risk assessments, attend information sessions, and deliver training to teams by end of year.
Deficit from operations before capital: -$20.6m in 2023. Total operating revenue $857.2m vs total operating expenditure $877.9m. Capital outflows $85.9m against net capital revenue $12.4m.

challenges.challenge: Infrastructure pressure from growing enrolments in Western Sydney
challenges.detail: 10-Year Capital Framework addresses rising enrolment. Business cases for 28 schools commenced late 2023. Growing demand for quality Catholic education in Western Sydney and Blue Mountains explicitly acknowledged.
Teacher Scholarship Program launched late 2023 targeting Year 12 students — part of a new 'Early Careers Engagement strategy'. Indicates recognition of future teacher supply risk.

raw_quotes.quote: Education is an unrelenting commitment to every child and we thank everyone at CSPD for their ongoing ministry of leadership and outreach.
raw_quotes.speaker: Elizabeth Crouch AM and Jack de Groot (joint welcome letter)

raw_quotes.quote: We move into 2024 with an even greater sense of common direction and purpose that will be crystallised in the development of a new organisation-wide strategy. Our focus will be on giving our students what they need to flourish, about their growth and development and that of each member of our staff, and striving for excellence in all things.
raw_quotes.speaker: Elizabeth Crouch AM and Jack de Groot (joint welcome letter)

raw_quotes.quote: The 2023 NAPLAN dataset has shed light on the imperative for CSPD to increase the transition of students from 'Strong' to 'Exceeding', particularly in the area of Numeracy. These insights from the 2023 NAPLAN data have significantly influenced and contributed to CSPD's priorities, encompassing the cultivation of a performance-driven culture, evidence-based explicit teaching methodologies, and the implementation of flexible learning environments.
raw_quotes.speaker: Annual Report (system statement)
raw_quotes.topic: NAPLAN gap — numeracy, teaching methodology, learning environments

raw_quotes.quote: All our schools place a very high value on academic achievement. Young people in Greater Western Sydney deserve the very best opportunities in life. Great HSC results are just one way of making sure that we give our students every option for the future.
raw_quotes.speaker: Jack de Groot, Executive Director of Schools

raw_quotes.quote: Stakeholder engagement remains a cornerstone of our planning process. We deeply value the support of our school leaders and look forward to working together as we fulfil the growing demand for quality Catholic education in Western Sydney and the Blue Mountains.
raw_quotes.speaker: Annual Report (infrastructure section)

raw_quotes.quote: In line with what our staff have told us, our areas of focus include co-designing an organisational strategy that incorporates the voices of staff and students, increasing the presence and visibility of our System Leaders, enhancing our communication, and launching initiatives that foster greater work-life balance and improved wellbeing for our staff.
raw_quotes.speaker: Annual Report (Employee Engagement Survey findings)

raw_quotes.quote: The Policy acknowledges that working in the mission of education, on behalf of the Catholic Church, is rewarding yet complex.
raw_quotes.speaker: Annual Report (Staff Wellbeing Policy description)
raw_quotes.topic: staff wellbeing — complexity of mission work

parramatta_stats.note: August 2024 CSNSW census data. CSPD 2023 report states 80 schools — difference may reflect additions between 2023 and August 2024.

funding.note_on_3bn: CSNSW is 'responsible for distributing recurrent funding in excess of $3 billion each year for diocesan school systems'

non_government_school_taxpayer_savings_r.source_publication: Public Savings from Non-Public Schools, KBRI, 2024

funding.funding_distribution_model: New FDM 2024-2029 approved by Bishop Members July 2023, first implemented January 2024.
funding.source: csnsw-annual-report-2024.pdf p.8 & p.15 & p.29 & p.52 & p.54

challenges.challenge: National School Reform Agreement negotiations unresolved
challenges.detail: Better and Fairer Schools Agreement (2025-2035) not finalised at end of 2024. NSW Government had not concluded negotiations with Commonwealth. NSRA extended a further year for NSW. Negotiations continue in 2025.

challenges.challenge: Threat to non-government school funding model
challenges.detail: CSNSW launched 'Keep School Funding Fair' campaign December 2024. Research shows lack of public understanding leaves funding model vulnerable to ideological attack ahead of 2025 Federal Election.

Inter-Diocesan Teacher Workforce Working Group met five times in 2024 coordinating response to National Teacher Workforce Action Plan. Recruitment and retention named as central theme at Sydney Morning Herald Schools Summit.

Governments continue trend of increased oversight. CSNSW as single Catholic registration authority since July 2023 — still maturing: 'The challenge moving forward will be the maturation of the CSNSW registration system over the next few years.'

ASIO escalated national terrorism threat level from 'possible' to 'probable' in August 2024. Substantial increase in reported incidents across education sector. CSNSW secured $2.8m NSW Government CVE funding through end of 2028.

challenges.challenge: Child protection complexity — rising student welfare demands
challenges.detail: Notable increase in support requests for student violence, self-harm, and problematic sexualised behaviour. Network also managing historical abuse claims.

NSW Department of Education advised that from July 2025 access to online assessments (including Year 1 Phonics Screening Check) will transition to cost-recovery model, potentially affecting non-government school voluntary participation. CSNSW advocating for equitable access.

Comprehensive curriculum reform 2020-2027. First reform in three decades. Schools implementing revised syllabuses simultaneously across K-6, 7-10, 11-12. Demand for online assessments expected to increase during reform period.

CSNSW consolidated deficit 2024: -$1,349,553 (vs surplus of $2,789,738 in 2023). Revenue fell from $42.1m to $38.7m (consolidated). 77% of revenue from government programs (84% in 2023) — high economic dependency.

challenges.challenge: School counselling — rising student mental health burden
challenges.detail: Key areas of concern in 2024: self-harm, suicidality, anxiety, depression, family and domestic violence issues.

parramatta_references.reference: Parramatta diocese: 45,646 students and 83 schools (August 2024) — second largest diocese in NSW after Sydney (73,853 students, 147 schools)
NSWCPS MacKillop group (primary sport): Parramatta contributes 676 students
John Gildea, CSPD — member of Inter-Diocesan Registration and Accreditation Committee (IDRAC)
Francis Mackay, CSPD — IDRAC proxy; member Inter-Diocesan Educational Accountability Group

CBGA capital grants 2024 — Parramatta diocese projects: St Andrew's Primary School Marayong ($4.2m total / $2.9m grant); Mary Immaculate Primary School Quakers Hill ($10.8m / $7.6m); St John's Primary School Riverstone ($3.4m / $2.4m); St John Paul II College Schofields ($16.5m / $11.6m)
Bishop Vincent Long Van Nguyen OFM Conv DD — listed as Bishop of Parramatta in CSNSW Bishop Members

Multiple CSPD staff active in CSNSW advisory groups: Georgina Vega and John Gildea (Transition/Pathways); John Wills (VET Advisory); Kim Grinham (VET Reference); Lisa Nash and Stefan Boffa (K-6 Curriculum Reform); Paul Develin and Attila Lendvai (Online Curriculum Assessment); Sonya Roy (Records Retention); Amruta Dhage, Gerard Giesekam and Shrey Chopra (Finance Working Group); Daniel Lynch and Megan Bisset (Teacher Workforce); Brenda Dora-Higgins and Francis Mackay (Educational Accountability); Julie Waddell (Aboriginal Education); Frank Chiment and Kelly Bauer (Catholic Secondary Schools Reference); Christine Howe (Education Policy, until March 2024)
